generatedSentence: On a remote, peripatetic outpost orbiting distant planets, a persistent intel-analyst, known only as 'The Amplifier,' watched the tectonic shifts of galactic sovereignty. His independent intellect allowed him to fathom the hidden network of a rogue warlord. He undertook a deep dive into the library's vast audio archives, seeking a sign of the warlord's true chronology. The uniforms of his unit were a constant reminder of their task; the data streams, almost weightless, were a gateway to understanding the complex architecture of their junctions. He knew another tester would soon watch his progress. This garrulous warlord, a brainless faker, used his lackey to delete any fingerprint of his apocalyptic plans for the outer planets, including a brutal rugby tourney. His judgy, madman persona was merely a cover for his persistent threats, jeopardizing acceptable peace. The lieutenant's loyalty was a mala, a deceptive charm, masking a heart of obsidian. He’d ordered a runaway jumper to harvest specific data, hidden in the cupboard, which he would afford through layaway, promising a grand joyride to his kin. Awkwardly, the training was insufficient, and the information remained locked. Suddenly, a hot, radial pulse, like a massive bonfire's heat, hit the facility, causing every unit to reel into overdrive. This tectonic shock, akin to a mountain's violent birth, was a calculated move by the warlord, a persistent echo of his will. The computers began to delete critical data, creating a hidden, apocalyptic 'nothing' where records once stood, making the entire network vulnerable. It was an unacceptable act, a brainless attack designed to sever their sovereignty, leaving only a dewdrop of hope. The team, refusing to disavow their mission, realized this was the vanguard of a final assault. Through the shattered atrium and down the debris-strewn hallway, they found the hidden treasure: a small, handcraft enamel wheelbarrow containing an obsidian nugget, glowing with residual energy, a key to unlock the true power. The setting sun painted the landscapes in hues of an ending, yet a new beginning. They chose to unite, their hearts beating as one, to nurture a new future, rather than delete their past. The valkyrie's call echoed a new chronology, a reminder that through persistent effort, even the most apocalyptic threats could be overcome. This was their delight, their true sovereignty, to ensure the sun would rise another day.
panelSentences: On a remote, peripatetic outpost orbiting distant planets, a persistent intel-analyst, known only as 'The Amplifier,' watched the tectonic shifts of galactic sovereignty. His independent intellect allowed him to fathom the hidden network of a rogue warlord. He undertook a deep dive into the library's vast audio archives, seeking a sign of the warlord's true chronology. The uniforms of his unit were a constant reminder of their task; the data streams, almost weightless, were a gateway to understanding the complex architecture of their junctions. He knew another tester would soon watch his progress.

This garrulous warlord, a brainless faker, used his lackey to delete any fingerprint of his apocalyptic plans for the outer planets, including a brutal rugby tourney. His judgy, madman persona was merely a cover for his persistent threats, jeopardizing acceptable peace. The lieutenant's loyalty was a mala, a deceptive charm, masking a heart of obsidian. He’d ordered a runaway jumper to harvest specific data, hidden in the cupboard, which he would afford through layaway, promising a grand joyride to his kin. Awkwardly, the training was insufficient, and the information remained locked.

Suddenly, a hot, radial pulse, like a massive bonfire's heat, hit the facility, causing every unit to reel into overdrive. This tectonic shock, akin to a mountain's violent birth, was a calculated move by the warlord, a persistent echo of his will. The computers began to delete critical data, creating a hidden, apocalyptic 'nothing' where records once stood, making the entire network vulnerable. It was an unacceptable act, a brainless attack designed to sever their sovereignty, leaving only a dewdrop of hope. The team, refusing to disavow their mission, realized this was the vanguard of a final assault.

Through the shattered atrium and down the debris-strewn hallway, they found the hidden treasure: a small, handcraft enamel wheelbarrow containing an obsidian nugget, glowing with residual energy, a key to unlock the true power. The setting sun painted the landscapes in hues of an ending, yet a new beginning. They chose to unite, their hearts beating as one, to nurture a new future, rather than delete their past. The valkyrie's call echoed a new chronology, a reminder that through persistent effort, even the most apocalyptic threats could be overcome. This was their delight, their true sovereignty, to ensure the sun would rise another day.
rawPanelSentences: On a remote, peripatetic outpost orbiting distant planets, a persistent intel-analyst, known only as 'The Amplifier,' watched the tectonic shifts of galactic sovereignty. His independent intellect allowed him to fathom the hidden network of a rogue warlord. He undertook a deep dive into the library's vast audio archives, seeking a sign of the warlord's true chronology. The uniforms of his unit were a constant reminder of their task; the data streams, almost weightless, were a gateway to understanding the complex architecture of their junctions. He knew another tester would soon watch his progress.

This garrulous warlord, a brainless faker, used his lackey to delete any fingerprint of his apocalyptic plans for the outer planets, including a brutal rugby tourney. His judgy, madman persona was merely a cover for his persistent threats, jeopardizing acceptable peace. The lieutenant's loyalty was a mala, a deceptive charm, masking a heart of obsidian. He’d ordered a runaway jumper to harvest specific data, hidden in the cupboard, which he would afford through layaway, promising a grand joyride to his kin. Awkwardly, the training was insufficient, and the information remained locked.

Suddenly, a hot, radial pulse, like a massive bonfire's heat, hit the facility, causing every unit to reel into overdrive. This tectonic shock, akin to a mountain's violent birth, was a calculated move by the warlord, a persistent echo of his will. The computers began to delete critical data, creating a hidden, apocalyptic 'nothing' where records once stood, making the entire network vulnerable. It was an unacceptable act, a brainless attack designed to sever their sovereignty, leaving only a dewdrop of hope. The team, refusing to disavow their mission, realized this was the vanguard of a final assault.

Through the shattered atrium and down the debris-strewn hallway, they found the hidden treasure: a small, handcraft enamel wheelbarrow containing an obsidian nugget, glowing with residual energy, a key to unlock the true power. The setting sun painted the landscapes in hues of an ending, yet a new beginning. They chose to unite, their hearts beating as one, to nurture a new future, rather than delete their past. The valkyrie's call echoed a new chronology, a reminder that through persistent effort, even the most apocalyptic threats could be overcome. This was their delight, their true sovereignty, to ensure the sun would rise another day.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. PROTAGONIST: A lean, cloaked figure with glowing green optic sensors and a cybernetic arm, holding a data-tablet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: PROTAGONIST, hunched over a holographic console projecting distant planets and data streams, viewed from a low angle, emphasizing the vastness of space. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A grotesque, judgy warlord figure, illuminated by the red glow of an explosion, his lackey cowering behind him, viewed from a slightly high angle looking down on the chaos. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: PROTAGONIST, leaping over fallen debris in a shattered futuristic atrium, a massive radial shockwave depicted behind him, viewed from a dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: PROTAGONIST, standing victoriously at sunset on a mountain landscape, holding a glowing obsidian nugget, a sense of calm and triumph, viewed from a wide, establishing shot.

definitions.definition: A sound, or a sound signal
Focused on audible sound, as opposed to sight.
A key that may be pressed to delete something (such as text or files) from a computer.
A deletion.
definitions.example: I lost the file when I accidentally hit delete.

definitions.definition: (recorded entertainment industry) A remainder of a music or video release.
To remove, get rid of or erase, especially written or printed material, or data on a computer or other device.
(with up) To heat; to make or become hot.
(with up) To become lively or exciting.
(of an object) Having a high temperature.
definitions.example: He forgot that the frying pan was hot and burned his hand.

definitions.definition: (of the weather) Causing the air to be hot.
definitions.example: It is hotter in summer than in winter.

definitions.definition: (of a person or animal) Feeling the sensation of heat, especially to the point of discomfort.
definitions.example: Aren't you hot with that thick coat on?

definitions.definition: (of a temper) Easily provoked to anger.
definitions.example: Be careful, he has a hot temper and may take it out on you.

definitions.definition: Feverish.
(of food) Spicy.
definitions.example: Before moving to India, I never ate hot food. The Indians love spicy food.

definitions.definition: Very good, remarkable, exciting.
definitions.example: He's a hot young player, we should give him a trial.

definitions.definition: Stolen.
definitions.example: hot merchandise

definitions.definition: Electrically charged.
definitions.example: a hot wire

definitions.definition: Radioactive.
(of a person) Very physically and/or sexually attractive.
definitions.example: That stripper is hot!

definitions.definition: Sexual or sexy; involving sexual intercourse or sexual excitement.
Sexually aroused; randy.
definitions.example: Enough foreplay! You’ve gotten me so hot already!

definitions.definition: (with for) Attracted to.
definitions.example: hot for her English teacher

definitions.definition: Popular; in demand.
definitions.example: This new pickup is so hot we can't keep it in stock!

definitions.definition: Of great current interest; provoking current debate or controversy.
definitions.example: a hot topic

definitions.definition: Very close to finding or guessing something to be found or guessed.
definitions.example: Am I warm yet? — You're hot!

definitions.definition: Performing strongly; having repeated successes.
Fresh; just released.
Uncomfortable, difficult to deal with; awkward, dangerous, unpleasant.
Used to emphasize the short duration or small quantity of something
definitions.example: He was finished in a hot minute.

definitions.definition: Characterized by police presence or activity.
definitions.example: I wouldn't speed through here if I was you. This area is hot this time of night.

definitions.definition: (of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.
definitions.example: I wouldn't trust him. He gave me a hot check last week.

definitions.definition: A small, one-wheeled (rarely two-wheeled) cart with handles at one end for transporting small loads.
To convey in a wheelbarrow.
To cause the weight of an aeroplane to become concentrated around the nosewheel.
The lowest Junior Commissioned Officer rank(s) in many military forces, often Army and Marines.
A person who manages or executes the plans and directives of another, more senior person - i.e. a manager to his director.
The second-in-command (2IC) of a group.
A military grade that is junior to the grade the adjective modifies: lieutenant colonel, lieutenant general, lieutenant commander.
A footman, a liveried male servant.
A fawning, servile follower.
To attend, wait upon, serve obsequiously.
To toady, play the flunky.
Each of the seven major bodies which move relative to the fixed stars in the night sky—the Moon, Mercury, Venus, the Sun, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn.

A body which orbits the Sun directly and is massive enough to be in hydrostatic equilibrium (effectively meaning a spheroid) and to dominate its orbit; specifically, the eight major bodies of Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. (Pluto was considered a planet until 2006 and has now been reclassified as a dwarf planet.)
A large body which directly orbits any star (or star cluster) but which has not attained nuclear fusion.
In phrases such as the planet, this planet, sometimes refers to the Earth.
Excessively or tiresomely talkative.
(of something written or performed) Excessively wordy and rambling.
An element of broadcast programming originating away from the station's or show's control room.
To connect to a computer from a remote location.
At a distance; disconnected.
definitions.example: A remote operator may control the vehicle with a wireless handset.

definitions.definition: Distant or otherwise inaccessible.
definitions.example: After his fall from the emperor's favor, the general was posted to a remote outpost.

definitions.definition: (especially with respect to likelihood) Slight.
definitions.example: There was only a remote possibility that we would be rescued as we were far outside of the regular shipping lanes.

definitions.definition: Emotionally detached.
definitions.example: After her mother's death, my friend grew remote for a time while she dealt with her grief.

definitions.definition: A device used to operate an appliance (such as a television), vehicle or mechanical toy from a short distance away.
A means of doing something from a distance.
definitions.example: The bomb was detonated by remote control.

definitions.definition: Inclined to make judgments; judgmental.
One who predicts apocalypse.
Of or relating to an apocalypse:
Portending a future apocalypse (disaster, devastation, or doom).
Eggcorn of apoplectic.
definitions.example: He was apocalyptically furious.

definitions.definition: Worthy, decent, sure of being accepted or received with at least moderate pleasure
definitions.example: We need to find an acceptable present for Jeff.

definitions.definition: Barely worthy, less than excellent; passable.
definitions.example: The designs were acceptable, but they were nothing special either.

definitions.definition: Tournament.
To take part in a tournament.
A small, compact chunk or clump.
definitions.example: a gold nugget

definitions.definition: A chicken nugget.
A tidbit of something valuable.
definitions.example: a nugget of wisdom

definitions.definition: A small piece of tasty food, a tidbit.
A type of boot polish.
A bud from the Cannabis sativa plant, especially one that is potent.
An inexperienced, newly trained fighter pilot.
A partial description gleaned from data mining.
Race; family; breed; kind.
Persons of the same race or family; kindred.
One or more relatives, such as siblings or cousins, taken collectively.
Relationship; same-bloodedness or affinity; near connection or alliance, as of those having common descent.
Kind; sort; manner; way.
Related by blood or marriage, akin. Generally used in "kin to".
definitions.example: It turns out my back-fence neighbor is kin to one of my co-workers.

definitions.definition: One who walks about; a pedestrian; an itinerant.
(usually capitalized) One who accepts the philosophy of Aristotle or his school; an Aristotelian.
Tending to walk about.
Constantly travelling; itinerant; nomadic.
(usually capitalized) Having to do with Aristotle, his philosophy, or the school of thought which he founded -- from the practice of conducting philosophical conversations while taking a walk.
Obstinately refusing to give up or let go.
definitions.example: She has had a persistent cough for weeks.

definitions.definition: Insistently repetitive.
definitions.example: There was a persistent knocking on the door.

definitions.definition: Indefinitely continuous.
definitions.example: There have been persistent rumours for years.

definitions.definition: Lasting past maturity without falling off.
definitions.example: Pine cones have persistent scales.

definitions.definition: Of data or a data structure: not transient or temporary, but remaining in existence after the termination of the program that creates it.
definitions.example: Once written to a disk file, the data becomes persistent: it will still be there tomorrow when we run the next program.

definitions.definition: Describing a fractal process that has a positive Brown function
(stochastic processes, of a state) non-transient.
A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
(of a polity) The state of making laws and controlling resources without the coercion of other nations.
(of a ruler) Supreme authority over all things.
(of a person) The liberty to decide one's thoughts and actions.
Anything that amplifies, or makes something larger or more intense.
An adverb that adds intensity, such as "really" or "totally".
An appliance or circuit that increases the strength of a weak electrical signal without changing the other characteristics of the signal.
A portable encasement that houses a large speaker, used to amplify voices and musical instruments at live performances.
A lens that enlarges the field of vision.
Of or relating to construction or to architecture
Structural
Of, relating to, or caused by large-scale movements of the Earth's lithosphere
Momentous, utter, vast
Which has no weight.
Which is not being affected by gravity.
The state of being married.

definitions.definition: A union of two or more people that creates a family tie and carries legal, social, and/or religious rights and responsibilities.
A homosexual relationship between male prisoners.
A wedding; a ceremony in which people wed.
definitions.example: You are cordially invited to the marriage of James Smith and Jane Doe.

definitions.definition: A close union.
A joining of two parts.
A king and a queen, when held as a hand in Texas hold 'em or melded in pinochle.
In solitaire or patience games, the placing a card of the same suit on the next one above or below it in value.
An institution which holds books and/or other forms of media for use by the public or qualified people often lending them out, as well as providing various other services for its users.
(by extension) Any institution that lends out its goods for use by the public or a community.
A collection of books or other forms of stored information.
An equivalent collection of analogous information in a non-printed form, e.g. record library.
A room dedicated to storing books.
A collection of software routines that provide functionality to be incorporated into or used by a computer program.
A collection of DNA material from a single organism or relative to a single disease.
The deck or draw pile.
The natural pattern of ridges on the tips of human fingers, unique to each individual.
The patterns left on surfaces where uncovered fingertips have touched, especially as used to identify the person who touched the surface.
Unique identification for public key in asymmetric cryptosystem.
A unique combination of features that serves as an identification of something.
A trace that gives evidence of someone's involvement.
To take somebody's fingerprints.

definitions.definition: To identify something uniquely by a combination of measurements.
Grasp, envelopment, control.
(now usually nautical) An English unit of length for water depth notionally based upon the width of grown man's outstretched arms but standardized as 6 feet (about 1.8 m).
Various similar units in other systems.
Depth of insight, mental reach or scope.
To encircle with outstretched arms, especially to take a measurement; to embrace.
To measure the depth of, take a sounding of.
To get to the bottom of; to manage to comprehend; understand (a problem etc.).

definitions.definition: Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.
A jumping spider
The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.
A nuclear power plant worker who repairs equipment in areas with extremely high levels of radiation.
To connect with an electrical jumper.
A wastebasket; a bin used for household or office waste.
A kind of fabric or blanket used in Latin America and southwestern United States.
Any of several very large pelagic rays of the genus Manta, with winglike pectoral fins, a long tail, and two fins resembling horns that project from the head.
A British gold coin worth 20 shillings, first produced during the reign of King James I, and bearing a legend indicating the king's intention of uniting the kingdoms of England and Scotland.
To bring together as one.
definitions.example: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.

definitions.definition: The time of day when the sun disappears below the western horizon.

definitions.definition: Having a set termination date.
definitions.example: The tax increase legislation included a sunset clause requiring renewal to prevent the tax increase from expiring.

definitions.definition: The region where the sun sets; the west.
To phase out.
definitions.example: We'll be sunsetting version 1.9 of the software shortly after releasing version 2.0 next quarter.

definitions.definition: A board or table used to openly hold and display silver plate and other dishware; a sideboard; a buffet.
Things displayed on a sideboard; dishware, particularly valuable plate.
A cabinet, closet, or other piece of furniture with shelves intended for storing cookware, dishware, or food; similar cabinets or closets used for storing other items.
definitions.example: Put the cups back into the cupboard.

definitions.definition: Things stored in a cupboard; particularly food.

(usually uncountable) A form of football in which players can hold or kick an ovoid ball. The ball cannot be handled forwards and points are scored by touching the ball to the ground in the area past their opponent’s territory or kicking the ball between goalposts and over a crossbar.
definitions.example: The scrum is a distinctive element of rugby.

definitions.definition: A loose fitting shirt with a collar, as worn by rugby players.
To wander or travel freely and with no specific destination.
To use a network or service from different locations or devices.
To transmit (resources) between different locations or devices, to allow comparable usage from any of them.
To range or wander over.

definitions.definition: An instance of wandering.
The ability to use a cell phone outside of its original registering zone.
The use of a network or service from different locations or devices.
(operating system) Using the OS service of manipulating folders and documents from different PC devices by a registered user.
Joy; pleasure.
Something that gives great joy or pleasure.
To give delight to; to affect with great pleasure; to please highly.
definitions.example: A beautiful landscape delights the eye.

definitions.definition: To have or take great pleasure.
To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
To be occupied bringing in a harvest
definitions.example: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

definitions.definition: To win, achieve a gain.
definitions.example: The rising star harvested well-deserved acclaim, even an Oscar under 21

definitions.definition: The gathering of a mature crop; a harvest.
(by extension) The gathering of any resource.
A slope.
definitions.example: To reach the building, we had to climb a steep incline.

definitions.definition: To bend or move (something) out of a given plane or direction, often the horizontal or vertical.
definitions.example: He had to incline his body against the gusts to avoid being blown down in the storm.

definitions.definition: To slope.
definitions.example: Over the centuries the wind made the walls of the farmhouse incline.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the passive) To tend to do or believe something, or move or be moved in a certain direction, away from a point of view, attitude, etc.
definitions.example: He inclines to believe anything he reads in the newspapers.

definitions.definition: To pick; to make the choice of; to select.
definitions.example: I chose a nice ripe apple from the fruit bowl.

definitions.definition: To elect.
definitions.example: He was chosen as president in 1990.

definitions.definition: To decide to act in a certain way.
definitions.example: I chose to walk to work today.

definitions.definition: To wish; to desire; to prefer.
The binomial coefficient of the previous and following number.
An opaque, glassy coating baked onto metal or ceramic objects.
A coating that dries to a hard, glossy finish.
The hard covering on the exposed part of a tooth.
A cosmetic intended to give the appearance of a smooth and beautiful complexion.
Located or positioned out of sight; not visually apparent.

definitions.definition: Obscure.
To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.
definitions.example: He hides his magazines under the bed.

definitions.definition: To put oneself in a place where one will be harder to find or out of sight.
To take upon oneself; to start, to embark on (a specific task etc.).
To commit oneself (to an obligation, activity etc.).
definitions.example: He undertook to exercise more in future.

definitions.definition: To overtake on the wrong side.
definitions.example: I hate people who try and undertake on the motorway.

definitions.definition: To pledge; to assert, assure; to dare say.
To take by trickery; to trap, to seize upon.
To assume, as a character; to take on.
To engage with; to attack, take on in a fight.
To have knowledge of; to hear.
To have or take charge of.
The action or process of graduating and receiving a diploma for completing a course of study (such as from an educational institution).
A commencement ceremony.
A marking (e.g., on a container) indicating a measurement, usually one of many such markings that are each separated by a constant interval.
The exposure of a liquid in large surfaces to the air, so as to hasten its evaporation.
To ingest; to be ingested.
To use up.
To cause (someone) to worry.

definitions.definition: To take the loss in a transaction.
To be injured or killed by (something such as a firearm or its projectile), especially in the mouth.
To corrode or erode.
definitions.example: The acid rain ate away the statue.  The strong acid eats through the metal.

definitions.definition: To perform oral sex (on a person or body part).
definitions.example: Eat me!

definitions.definition: (especially in combination) That has been consumed by eating
A massage of the back.
The act of unlocking something.
To undo or open a lock or something locked by, for example, turning a key, or selecting a combination.
definitions.example: I unlocked the door and walked in.

definitions.definition: To obtain access to something.
definitions.example: I unlocked the dictionary article so I could edit it.

definitions.definition: To disclose or reveal previously unknown knowledge.
definitions.example: The discovery of a clue unlocked the mystery.

definitions.definition: To be or become unfastened or unrestrained.
(sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.
definitions.example: I gave them a thumbs-up sign.

definitions.definition: Physical evidence left by an animal.
definitions.example: The hunters found deer sign at the end of the trail.

definitions.definition: A clearly visible object, generally flat, bearing a short message in words or pictures.
definitions.example: I missed the sign at the corner so I took the wrong turn.

definitions.definition: A wonder; miracle; prodigy.
An astrological sign.
definitions.example: Your sign is Taurus? That's no surprise.

definitions.definition: Positive or negative polarity, as denoted by the + or - sign.
definitions.example: I got the magnitude right, but the sign was wrong.

definitions.definition: A specific gesture or motion used to communicate by those with speaking or hearing difficulties; now specifically, a linguistic unit in sign language equivalent to word in spoken languages.
Sign language in general.
definitions.example: Sorry, I don't know sign very well.

definitions.definition: An omen.
definitions.example: "It's a sign of the end of the world," the doom prophet said.

definitions.definition: A property of the body that indicates a disease and, unlike a symptom, is unlikely to be noticed by the patient.
A military emblem carried on a banner or standard.
Any of various venomous American snakes, of genera Crotalus and Sistrurus, having a rattle at the end of its tail.
A shaky or unsteady gait.
A lively dance originating in Scotland; also, the music of this dance; often called a Scottish (or Scotch) reel.
A kind of spool, turning on an axis, on which yarn, threads, lines, or the like, are wound.

definitions.definition: A machine on which yarn is wound and measured into lays and hanks, —-- for cotton or linen it is fifty-four inches in circuit; for worsted, thirty inches.
A device consisting of radial arms with horizontal stats, connected with a harvesting machine, for holding the stalks of grain in position to be cut by the knives.
A short compilation of sample film work used as a demonstrative resume in the entertainment industry.
To wind on a reel.
To spin or revolve repeatedly.
To unwind, to bring or acquire something by spinning or winding something else.
definitions.example: He reeled off some tape from the roll and sealed the package.

definitions.definition: To walk shakily or unsteadily; to stagger; move as if drunk or not in control of oneself.
(with back) To back off or step away unsteadily and quickly.
definitions.example: He reeled back from the punch.

definitions.definition: To make or cause to reel.
To have a whirling sensation; to be giddy.
To be in shock.
To roll.
To drive too hard, or far, or beyond strength.
A central room or space in ancient Roman homes, open to the sky in the middle; a similar space in other buildings.
A square hall lit by daylight from above, into which rooms open at one or more levels.
A cavity, entrance, or passage.
Any enclosed sexine and nexine layers, widening toward the interior of the grain.
A muscular organ that pumps blood through the body, traditionally thought to be the seat of emotion.
Emotions, kindness, moral effort, or spirit in general.
definitions.example: The team lost, but they showed a lot of heart.

definitions.definition: The seat of the affections or sensibilities, collectively or separately, as love, hate, joy, grief, courage, etc.; rarely, the seat of the understanding or will; usually in a good sense; personality.
definitions.example: a good, tender, loving, bad, hard, or selfish heart

definitions.definition: Courage; courageous purpose; spirit.
Vigorous and efficient activity; power of fertile production; condition of the soil, whether good or bad.
A term of affectionate or kindly and familiar address.
definitions.example: Listen, dear heart, we must go now.

definitions.definition: Personality, disposition.
definitions.example: a cold heart

definitions.definition: A wight or being.
A conventional shape or symbol used to represent the heart, love, or emotion: ♥ or sometimes <3.
A playing card of the suit hearts featuring one or more heart-shaped symbols.
The twenty-fourth Lenormand card.
The centre, essence, or core.
definitions.example: Buddhists believe that suffering is right at the heart of all life.

definitions.definition: To be fond of. Often bracketed or abbreviated with a heart symbol.
definitions.example: 2008 July 25, "The Media Hearts Obama?", On The Media, National Public Radio

definitions.definition: To give heart to; to hearten; to encourage.
To fill an interior with rubble, as a wall or a breakwater.
To form a dense cluster of leaves, a heart, especially of lettuce or cabbage.
One of the four suits of playing cards, in red, marked with the symbol ♥.
A trick-taking card game in which players are penalized for taking hearts and (especially) the queen of spades.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
A building founded for the long-term care of its residents, such as an almshouse. The residents may have no physical ailments, but simply need financial support.
A place of lodging.
A high military officer in a warlike nation.
A local ruler or bandit leader usually where the government is weak.
The image-making power of the mind; the act of mentally creating or reproducing an object not previously perceived; the ability to create such images.
definitions.example: Imagination is one of the most advanced human faculties.

definitions.definition: Particularly, construction of false images; fantasizing.
definitions.example: You think someone's been following you? That's just your imagination.

definitions.definition: Creativity; resourcefulness.
definitions.example: His imagination makes him a valuable team member.

definitions.definition: A mental image formed by the action of the imagination as a faculty; something imagined.
Any plant of the genus Helianthus, so called probably from the form and color of its floral head, having the form of a large disk surrounded by yellow ray flowers.
A bright yellow, like that of the flower petals.
Any flat, radially symmetric organic compound such as coronene
The act or process of absorbing or of being absorbed as,
Entire engrossment or occupation of the mind.
definitions.example: absorption in some employment

definitions.definition: Mental assimilation.
The retaining of electrical energy for a short time after it has been introduced to the dielectric.
An additional one of the same kind.
definitions.example: There is one sterling and here is another

definitions.definition: One that is different from the current one.
definitions.example: I saw one movie, but I think I will see another.

definitions.definition: One of a group of things of the same kind.
definitions.example: His interests keep shifting from one thing to another.

definitions.definition: To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious;—with an auxiliary, as can, could, might, etc.; to be able or rich enough.
definitions.example: I think we can afford the extra hour it will take.  We can only afford to buy a small car at the moment.

definitions.definition: To offer, provide, or supply, as in selling, granting, expending, with profit, or without loss or too great injury.
definitions.example: A affords his goods cheaper than B.

definitions.definition: To give forth; to supply, yield, or produce as the natural result, fruit, or issue.
definitions.example: Grapes afford wine.  Olives afford oil.  The earth affords fruit.  The sea affords an abundant supply of fish.

definitions.definition: To give, grant, or confer, with a remoter reference to its being the natural result; to provide; to furnish.
definitions.example: A good life affords consolation in old age.

definitions.definition: In an awkward manner; with discomfort or lack of coordination.
A canopy over a bed.
Something that overhangs something else; especially a canopy or soundboard over a pulpit.
Authorization or certification; a sanction, as given by a superior.
Something that provides assurance or confirmation; a guarantee or proof.
definitions.example: a warrant of authenticity; a warrant for success

definitions.definition: An order that serves as authorization; especially a voucher authorizing payment or receipt of money.
An option, usually issued together with another security and with a term at issue greater than a year, to buy other securities of the issuer.
A judicial writ authorizing an officer to make a search, seizure, or arrest, or to execute a judgment.
definitions.example: an arrest warrant issued by the court

definitions.definition: Short for warrant officer.
A document certifying that a motor vehicle meets certain standards of mechanical soundness and safety; a warrant of fitness.
A defender, a protector.
Underclay in a coal mine.
To protect, keep safe (from danger).
To give (someone) an assurance or guarantee (of something); also, with a double object: to guarantee (someone something).
To guarantee (something) to be (of a specified quality, value, etc.).
To guarantee as being true; (colloquially) to believe strongly.
definitions.example: That tree is going to fall, I’ll warrant.

definitions.definition: To authorize; to give (someone) sanction or warrant (to do something).
definitions.example: I am warranted to search these premises fully.

definitions.definition: To justify; to give grounds for.
definitions.example: Circumstances arose that warranted the use of lethal force.

definitions.definition: Having no brain.
Unintelligent, with little common sense.
A radial tire / radial tyre.
Arranged like rays that radiate from, or converge to a common centre.
Moving along a radius.
Of, or relating to the radius bone.
Of, or relating to the radius (vein), and/or the wing areas next to it.
Developing uniformly on all sides.
Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.
A trivial remark (especially in the term sweet nothings).
A nobody (insignificant person).
definitions.example: You're nothing to me now!

definitions.definition: Not at all; in no way.
Not any thing; no thing.
An absence of anything, including empty space, brightness, darkness, matter, or a vacuum.
Things which have had a deposit placed on them and which the owner (generally a store) has agreed to hold for the customer and accept full payment at a later time.
The system of accepting a deposit and holding for a customer to complete purchase.
To place items on hold with a deposit at a merchant's.
To separate, especially by social policies that directly or indirectly keep races or ethnic groups apart.
(of a person or thing) Separated or isolated from others, or from another group.
(of an institution) Having access restricted to certain groups, or excluding certain groups.
One who fakes something.
An impostor or impersonator.
A thief.
A peddler of petty things.
A workman who dresses things up.
An instance of driving a motor vehicle in a carefree or reckless manner, especially a vehicle which has been taken without the permission of the owner.
A ride taken for enjoyment.
To take a joyride.
To put in jeopardy, to threaten.
definitions.example: After having an affair with a junior, her chances of promotion were seriously jeopardized.

definitions.definition: The leading units at the front of an army or fleet.
(by extension) The person(s) at the forefront of any group or movement.
A droplet of water formed as dew.
(1800s) A slow pitch.
Hell.
definitions.example: You can go to heck as far as I'm concerned.

definitions.definition: Hell.
definitions.example: What the heck are you doing?

definitions.definition: Resembling a willow.
(of a person) Tall, slender and graceful.
(of a place) Having willow trees.
A male who is insane or mentally disturbed.
A candidate or voter not affiliated with any political party, a freethinker, free of a party platform.
A neutral or uncommitted person.
A team not affiliated with any league or conference.
Not dependent; not contingent or depending on something else; free.
Not affiliated with any political party.
definitions.example: the independent candidate

definitions.definition: Providing a comfortable livelihood.
definitions.example: an independent property

definitions.definition: Not subject to bias or influence; self-directing.
definitions.example: a man of an independent mind

definitions.definition: Separate from; exclusive; irrespective.
The faculty of thinking, judging, abstract reasoning, and conceptual understanding; the cognitive faculty (uncountable)
definitions.example: Intellect is one of man's greatest powers.

definitions.definition: The capacity of that faculty (in a particular person) (uncountable)
definitions.example: They were chosen because of their outstanding intellect.

definitions.definition: A person who has that faculty to a great degree
definitions.example: Some of the world's leading intellects were meeting there.

definitions.definition: A person or animal that runs away or has run away; a person, animal, or organization that escapes captivity or restrictions.
definitions.example: Runaway children are vulnerable to criminal exploitation.

definitions.definition: A vehicle (especially, a train) that is out of control.
(usually attributive) An object or process that is out of control or out of equilibrium.
The act of running away, especially of a horse or teams.
An overwhelming victory.
definitions.example: The home side won in a runaway.

definitions.definition: Having run away; escaped; fugitive
definitions.example: a runaway thief

definitions.definition: Easily won, as a contest
definitions.example: a runaway victory at the polls

definitions.definition: Unchecked; rampant
definitions.example: runaway prices

definitions.definition: Deserting or revolting against one's group, duties, expected conduct, or the like, especially to establish or join a rival group, change one's life drastically, etc.
definitions.example: The runaway delegates nominated their own candidate.

definitions.definition: To strongly and solemnly refuse to own or acknowledge; to deny responsibility for, approbation of, and the like.
definitions.example: He was charged with embezzlement, but he disavows the crime.

definitions.definition: To deny; to show the contrary of; to deny legitimacy or achievement of any kind.
definitions.example: Because of her dissatisfaction, she now disavows the merits of fascism.

definitions.definition: To practice an ability.
definitions.example: She trained seven hours a day to prepare for the Olympics.

definitions.definition: To teach and form (someone) by practice; to educate (someone).
definitions.example: You can't train a pig to write poetry.

definitions.definition: To improve one's fitness.
definitions.example: I trained with weights all winter.

definitions.definition: To proceed in sequence.
To move (a gun) laterally so that it points in a different direction.
definitions.example: The assassin had trained his gun on the minister.

definitions.definition: To encourage (a plant or branch) to grow in a particular direction or shape, usually by pruning and bending.
definitions.example: The vine had been trained over the pergola.

definitions.definition: To trace (a lode or any mineral appearance) to its head.
To create a trainer for; to apply cheats to (a game).
To draw along; to trail; to drag.
To draw by persuasion, artifice, or the like; to attract by stratagem; to entice; to allure.
Action of the verb to train.
The activity of imparting and acquiring skills.
The result of good social upbringing.
The process by which two modems determine which protocol and speed to use; handshaking.
(voice recognition) The recording of multiple samples of a user's voice to aid pattern recognition.
A corridor in a building that connects rooms.
The act of nourishing or nursing; tender care
That which nourishes; food; diet.
The environmental influences that contribute to the development of an individual (as opposed to "nature").
To nourish or nurse.
(by extension) To encourage, especially the growth or development of something.
An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.
definitions.example: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.

definitions.definition: Something very large in size or quantity; a huge amount; a great heap.
definitions.example: He was a real mountain of a man, standing seven feet tall.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, relating to, or found on a mountain; like a mountain in size; (of living things) growing or living on a mountain.
definitions.example: some mountain ranges; the high mountain top; a scenic mountain view; the mountain lodge; the leaves of mountain cranberries; a mountain goat

definitions.definition: A difficult task or challenge.
A woman's large breast.
The twenty-first Lenormand card.
Wine made from grapes that grow on a mountain.
A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.
A sociological aspect of a physical area.
A picture representing a real or imaginary scene by land or sea, the main subject being the general aspect of nature, as fields, hills, forests, water, etc.
The pictorial aspect of a country.
A mode of printing where the horizontal sides are longer than the vertical sides
A space, indoor or outdoor and natural or man-made (as in "designed landscape")
A situation that is presented, a scenario
definitions.example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years

definitions.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
The act of joining, or the state of being joined.
A place where two things meet, especially where two roads meet.
The boundary between two physically different materials, especially between conductors, semiconductors, or metals.
The place where a distributary departs from the main stream.
A place where two or more railways or railroads meet.
A point in time between two unrelated consecutive broadcasts.
A kind of symbolic link to a directory.
In the Raku programming language, a construct representing a composite of several values connected by an operator.
A collection of valuable things; accumulated wealth; a stock of money, jewels, etc.
Anything greatly valued.
A term of endearment.
(of a person or thing) To consider to be precious; to value highly.
definitions.example: Oh, this ring is beautiful! I’ll treasure it forever.

definitions.definition: To store or stow in a safe place.
To enrich.
A fabric or structure of fibrous elements attached to each other at regular intervals.
Any interconnected group or system
definitions.example: A network of roads crisscrossed the country.

definitions.definition: A directory of people maintained for their advancement
definitions.example: To get a job in today's economy, it is important to have a strong network.

definitions.definition: A group of affiliated television stations that broadcast common programs from a parent company.
Multiple computers and other devices connected together to share information
definitions.example: The copy machine is connected to the network so it can now serve as a printer.

definitions.definition: To interact socially for the purpose of getting connections or personal advancement.
definitions.example: Many people find it worthwhile to network for jobs and information.

definitions.definition: To connect two or more computers or other computerized devices.
definitions.example: If we network his machine to the server, he will be able to see all the files.

definitions.definition: To interconnect a group or system.
To broadcast across an entire network of stations and affiliates at the same time.
A single lobe of an insect's maxilla.
The grinding surface of an insect's mandible.
The state of being loyal; fidelity.
Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause or nation.
Any system by which time is divided into days, weeks, months, and years.
definitions.example: The three principal calendars are the Gregorian, Jewish, and Islamic calendars.

definitions.definition: A means to determine the date consisting of a document containing dates and other temporal information.
definitions.example: Write his birthday on the calendar hanging on the wall.

definitions.definition: A list of planned events.
definitions.example: The club has a busy calendar this year.

definitions.definition: An orderly list or enumeration of persons, things, or events; a schedule.
definitions.example: a calendar of bills presented in a legislative assemblly;  a calendar of causes arranged for trial in court

definitions.definition: An appointment book (US), appointment diary (UK)
To set a date for a proceeding in court, usually done by a judge at a calendar call.
definitions.example: The judge agreed to calendar a hearing for pretrial motions for the week of May 15, but did not agree to calendar the trial itself on a specific date.

definitions.definition: To enter or write in a calendar; to register.
A type of black glass produced by volcanoes.
Black
The art and science of designing and managing the construction of buildings and other structures, particularly if they are well proportioned and decorated.
definitions.example: Architecture is the visual public expression of a culture’s achievements, values, and outlook. ― Max Roscoe, "How Your City Is Killing You With Ugliness"

definitions.definition: The profession of an architect.
Any particular style of building design.
Construction, in a more general sense; frame or structure; workmanship.
A unifying structure.
A specific model of a microchip or CPU.
definitions.example: The Intel architectures have more software written for them.

definitions.definition: The structure and design of a system or product.
definitions.example: The architecture of the company's billing system is designed to support its business goals.

definitions.definition: A polygon with three sides and three angles.
A set square.
A percussion instrument made by forming a metal rod into a triangular shape which is open at one angle. It is suspended from a string and hit with a metal bar to make a resonant sound.
(cue sports) A triangular piece of equipment used for gathering the balls into the formation required by the game being played.
A love triangle.
(systemics) The structure of systems composed with three interrelated objects.
A draughtsman's square in the form of a right-angled triangle.
A frame formed of three poles stuck in the ground and united at the top, to which people were bound when undergoing corporal punishment.
Any of various large papilionid butterflies of the genus Graphium.
A triangular formation of railway tracks, with a curve on at least one side.
Handicraft
The class of subjects for study that rely upon experimentation and observation.
To engage in handcraft or handicraft.
A portable or wearable timepiece.
definitions.example: More people today carry a watch on their wrists than in their pockets.

definitions.definition: The act of guarding and observing someone or something.
A particular time period when guarding is kept.
definitions.example: The second watch of the night began at midnight.

definitions.definition: A person or group of people who guard.
definitions.example: The watch stopped the travelers at the city gates.

definitions.definition: The post or office of a watchman; also, the place where a watchman is posted, or where a guard is kept.
A group of sailors and officers aboard a ship or shore station with a common period of duty: starboard watch, port watch.
A period of time on duty, usually four hours in length; the officers and crew who tend the working of a vessel during the same watch. (FM 55–501).
The act of seeing, or viewing, for a period of time.
A fire in which bones are burned.
A fire to burn unwanted or disreputable items or people: proscribed books, heretics etc.
A large, controlled outdoor fire, as a signal or to celebrate something.
To fire (pottery) using a bonfire.
To make, or celebrate around, a bonfire.

A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.
Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
A standard measure of a quantity.
definitions.example: The centimetre is a unit of length.

definitions.definition: The number one.
An organized group comprising people and/or equipment.
definitions.example: He was a member of a special police unit.

definitions.definition: A member of a military organization.
definitions.example: The fifth tank brigade moved in with 20 units. (i.e., 20 tanks)

definitions.definition: Any military element whose structure is prescribed by competent authority, such as a table of organization and equipment; specifically, part of an organization.
An organization title of a subdivision of a group in a task force.
A standard or basic quantity into which an item of supply is divided, issued or detailed. In this meaning, also called unit of issue.

With regard to Reserve Components of the Armed Forces, denotes a Selected Reserve unit organized, equipped, and trained for mobilization to serve on active duty as a unit or to augment or be augmented by another unit. Headquarters and support functions without wartime missions are not considered units.
The identity element, neutral element.
An element having an inverse, an invertible element; an associate of the unity.
In an adjunction, a natural transformation from the identity functor of the domain of the left adjoint functor to the composition of the right adjoint functor with the left adjoint functor.

A volume of rock or ice of identifiable origin and age range that is defined by the distinctive and dominant, easily mapped and recognizable petrographic, lithologic or paleontologic features (facies) that characterize it.
An item which may be sold singly.
definitions.example: We shipped nearly twice as many units this month as last month.

definitions.definition: A unit of alcohol.
One kilowatt-hour (as recorded on an electricity meter).

A measure of housing equivalent to the living quarters of one household; an apartment where a group of apartments is contained in one or more multi-storied buildings or a group of dwellings is in one or more single storey buildings, usually arranged around a driveway.
A gold coin of the reign of James I, worth twenty shillings.
A work unit.
A physically large person.
An entrance capable of being blocked by use of a gate.
Any point that represents the beginning of a transition from one place or phase to another.
A point at which freight moving from one territory to another is interchanged between transportation lines.

(digital communications) In wireless internet, an access point with additional software capabilities such as providing NAT and DHCP, which may also provide VPN support, roaming, firewalls, various levels of security, etc.
(digital communications) To make available via a gateway, or access point.
The science of determining the order in which events occurred.
An arrangement of events into chronological order; called a timeline when involving graphical elements.
Any of the female attendants of Odin, figures said to guide fallen warriors from the battlefield to Valhalla.
definitions.example: Wagner's “Der Ring des Nibelungen” (1853) famously features valkyries.

definitions.definition: A bridge with several spans that carries road or rail traffic over a valley or other obstacles.
